mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity risk-off positioning and negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of months where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 55% of observed quarters, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate revenue visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 20-day returns in over 60% of cases during low-inflation periods, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real personal consumption expenditures exceeding 0.5% signals weakening consumer spending that immediately pressures equity valuations and produces negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of months where RSAFS drops exceed 0.5%, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate creates a negative real interest rate environment that compresses equity discount factors and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and flat FEDFUNDS produces negative 60-day returns in over 55% of quarters, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls during late-cycle expansions signals robust employment that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in late-cycle regimes, with accuracy dropping below 50% or Rank IC falling below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in industrial production indicate expanding economic output and higher corporate profit expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of observations, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.
